title: [PAPER] [2602.23976] Large-scale portfolio optimization on a trapped-ion quantum computer
why:
- The study presents an end-to-end pipeline for large-scale portfolio selection with cardinality constraints, experimentally demonstrated on trapped-ion quantum processors.
- The approach uses a hardware-aware decomposition strategy, mapping financial portfolio optimization problems into quadratic unconstrained binary optimization (QUBO) subproblems suitable for quantum computing.
- Subproblems are solved on a 64-qubit Barium trapped-ion system, using the bias-field digitized counterdiabatic quantum optimization (BF-DCQO) method, which is non-variational and avoids classical parameter-training loops.
- Benchmarking on a 250-asset universe shows that larger executable subproblem sizes on quantum hardware reduce decomposition error and systematically improve solution quality, including better risk–return trade-offs compared to randomized baselines.
- The results establish a hardware-tested, scalable quantum workflow for applied financial optimization, linking available qubit resources to solution quality in realistic use cases.
